description_long: This is a pair of rustic wooden cabins, each featuring a gable roof covered in dark shingles and a prominent central chimney. The cabins are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving them a weathered, natural appearance. One cabin has a small covered porch with a wooden overhang supported by posts, while the other has a partially collapsed porch roof and a door that appears to have fallen off its hinges. Windows are minimal and small, with one visible on the side of each cabin, contributing to a simple, utilitarian design. The rear of one cabin displays a patch of white discoloration or possible moss growth on the chimney. The overall look is that of traditional, rural American cabins, suitable for a historical or rustic urban neighborhood setting.
description: A pair of rustic wooden cabins with gable shingle roofs, central chimneys, and small porches, showing signs of weathering and disrepair.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable ends of both cabins, with the porches and entrances visible; the nearer cabin's porch is intact, while the farther one appears partially collapsed.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the length of the cabins, the small side windows, and the fallen door and collapsed porch roof on the right cabin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back gable ends, central chimneys, and a patch of white discoloration on the farther cabin's chimney.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain wooden walls with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the cabins' simple construction.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in dark asphalt shingles, featuring a central brick chimney
attribute_window_arrangement: Each cabin has a single small rectangular window on the side wall, asymmetrically placed, with no visible windows on the front or rear facades
attribute_door_placement: Each cabin has one exterior door located on the front facade, with one door intact and the other fallen off; both are adjacent to the porch structures
